text: Argo preview. After your first pipeline run, open a browser-based viewer to iterate on voiceover, overlays, and timing without re-recording a single frame.

overlay.title: Edit. Preview. Ship.
overlay.body: Tweak voiceover, overlays, and timing without re-recording.

text: Your recorded video plays right in the browser, with aligned audio synced to the timeline. Scrub anywhere to jump to any moment.

overlay.text: Video and audio play in sync — scrub the timeline to jump around

text: Every scene from your demo appears in the sidebar. Click any scene card to jump directly to it. The active scene highlights automatically as the video plays.

overlay.text: Click any scene to jump to it

text: Edit voiceover text right in the browser. Change a word, rephrase a sentence, fix pronunciation. The save button turns amber when you have unsaved changes.

overlay.text: Edit voiceover text inline — save when you’re ready

text: Swap overlay templates and zones with simple dropdowns. Switch from a lower-third to a headline card, move it from bottom center to top right. The preview updates live as you type.

overlay.text: Swap overlay templates and zones with dropdowns

text: Hit Regen TTS on any scene to regenerate just that clip. No need to re-run the entire pipeline for a single word change. Surgical updates in seconds.

overlay.title: Regen TTS
overlay.body: Regenerate just one scene — no need to re-run the full pipeline.

text: Hit Save and your changes write directly to your scenes manifest on disk. Or hit Re-record to run the full pipeline with your edits and get a fresh video.

overlay.text: Save persists to disk — Re-record runs the full pipeline

text: Toggle audio and overlay visibility with the switches. Preview the video with or without narration, with or without overlays, however you like.

overlay.text: Toggle audio and overlays on or off

text: That's Argo preview. Edit, regen, save, repeat. All from your browser. Try it with npx argo preview.

overlay.title: npx argo preview my-demo
overlay.body: Iterate on voiceover, overlays, and timing — all from your browser.
